rulings.Case Title: COMMONWEALTH LAND TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC et al (PROVIDE ACCESS)
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 1: CROSS DEFENDANT FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT: GRANTED, no opposition filed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KEITH GORLEN et al VS. WARREN P MESSINEO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 2: Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order: DENIED in part, hearing required in part. Plaintiffs' requests per the Ex-Parte Application filed on May 2, 2014 are divided into two sets. Set One includes requests a) through f). Set Two includes requests (a) and (b). As to Set One: 1. Preliminary injunction is denied as to request "a)" as this request is too broad ["a) Recoding any amendment to the CCRs of the HOA"]; 2. Preliminary Injunction is DENIED as to requests "b)" and "c)" as the moving party failed to show inadequacy of the legal remedies and irreparable harm ["b) Taking any further sums from the HOA's reserve account unless in strict accord with CCP 5515; c) Taking any action to implement the "immediate rule" adopted by the HOA on April 23, 2014 appointing Theo Albers as Election Inspector for the HOA, or to implement the resolution passed that same day naming his as Election Inspector" ]; 3. Hearing required as to requests d) through f) to address the recordation of the amended CCRs in light of Villa de Las Palmas HOA v. Terifaj (2004) 33 Cal.4th 73. As to Set Two: Requests (a) and (b) are denied as these issues should be addressed in AAA proceedings, i.e. payment of AAA legal fees incurred in AAA proceeding; and moving party failed to show inadequacy of the legal remedies and irreparable harm. ["(a) Defendant Shattuck to indemnify the HOA for all costs and legal fees incurred in AAA; (b) Defendant Messineo and Shattuck replenish $5,000 wrongfully taken from the HOA's reserve account and put into the operating account to pay the HOA counsel retainer."] = (LATE TENTATIVE) (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. ALISHA MATHEWS GARY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Vacate Default And Default Judgment And Recall Writ Of Execution
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANT ALISHA GARY's Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment And Recall Writ Of Execution: GRANTED, subject to (1) Defendant paying $2,235 to Plaintiff no later than June 16, 2014 to bring Defendant current under the Settlement Agreement (amount is based on Defendant's rent being $185/month from September 2013 to June 2014); and (2) Settlement Agreement is reinstated until $1,673 is paid off in full. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE SAM MAZZA FOUNDATION VS. JEFF SHELTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Discovery Mtn For Order Compelling Further Answrs, To Def'S Form Interrogatories, Set One, Propounded On Pltf; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf The Sam Mazza Foundation In The Amount Of $1510
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT JEFF SHELTON's Motion For Order Compelling Further Answrs, To Def'S Form Interrogatories, Set One, Propounded On Plaintiff; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff The Sam Mazza Foundation In The Amount Of $1510: DENIED. Responses to Form Interrogatory 75.2 are in compliance with the Discovery Act to date. However, Plaintiff has an ongoing duty to supplement the responses when additional responsive information is obtained. = (501/REQ)
